content:
- Ramki Gaddipati Office Hours – Structured Summary for Digital Twin

- II. Overall Context of the Conversation/Call

- This meeting served as a comprehensive check-in on various ongoing operational, technical, and process initiatives across Zeta’s Processing Services, with strong focus on maintaining operational discipline, increasing platform independence (decoupling), and enhancing the automation of reporting and control functions. Ramki anchored the call by reviewing updates on customer/account servicing features (statements), the operationalization and reporting structure of the Platform Support Services team, reconciliation improvements, and Project Tutela progress. There was a stress on embedding operational checklists/reporting within core Zeta systems (OPS Center) and ensuring all bank/customer requirements are reflected and auditable within automation frameworks. Several exceptions, escalation protocols, and data visualization/reporting practices were reviewed, as well as the transition of responsibility across teams for key program areas, notably UPI and reconciliation modules.

- IV. Key Takeaways from the Call

- Platform Support Services Team Evolution:
- The team’s structure and role expansion are under active review and documentation, moving beyond mere EOD/troubleshooting. (Discussion on incubation, overlaps)

- Clean Room Banking Requirement Highlighted:
- There’s a need for strong physical and logical segregation (clean room) for select bank clients—Ramki reinforced the need for isolation and clarity before commitments.

- Statement Module Decoupling Direction:
- Ramki emphasized making the statements module fully independent from Pixel, ensuring zero third-party (Pixel) code dependencies in the statement dispatch path.

- Operational Lifecycle Accountability:
- Ramki called for the statement module to handle delivery, bounce tracking, and systemic exception scenario management as core functions—beyond simple report generation.

- OPS Center as the Operative Rhythm:
- Strong directive for embedding daily operational checklists/product metric reviews directly into the OPS Center to introduce process diligence and accountability for all clients.

- Escalation Protocols for Service Gaps:
- Ramki mandated that exceptions (e.g., delayed UPI reversals) should have clear escalation paths, insistence on concrete acknowledgements from all levels, and call-outs when vendor obligations aren’t being met.

- Reconciliation & Reporting Automation Advanced:
- Progress declared in automating load money/data churning, recon modules, and linking exceptions to actionable, auditable flows—extensible to further fund-flow modules.

- Demand-Driven Platform Scope:
- Ramki set the tone: platform teams should be pressured to deliver required exception/aging/reporting views on demand, with expectations raised accordingly.

- New Standard for Demonstrating Progress:
- Emphasis on visually communicating status and momentum (via dashboards/funnels/aging), with logical states and continuous progress reflected for both customers and internal leadership.

- Scalable Automation & Conceptual Grounding:
- Ramki encouraged a conceptual approach, not just bank-driven custom features, and advocated for internally defined terminology/theory in product documentation and demos.

- V. Ramki's Insights

1. On Team Structure and Clean Room Requirements:
- Ramki approaches organizational design with a focus on accountability and clarity—pressing for explicit ownership (who is leading requirement discussions), advising against overcommitting prior to thorough understanding, and emphasizing physical/logical isolation for regulatory/bank requirements.

2. Modular Decoupling and Ownership:
- Through a tone of certainty and instruction, Ramki mandated the decoupling of the statement module from Pixel, aiming for complete autonomy powered by Tachyon and Luminos. His clarity on zero third-party code reflects a principle of platform independence.

3. Operational Discipline as Culture:
- Ramki’s stress on embedding reviews and checklists in OPS Center, capped by metaphors (e.g., nurses and ICU checklists), underlines his belief that process rigor and daily habits are the backbone of reliable operations—it’s about “mindless diligence” as routine, not optional.

4. Vision for Reporting and Exception Handling:
- Ramki repeatedly challenges the team to elevate their expectations from platforms—automation should deliver immediate, granular views (transaction aging, uncleared authorizations, unresolved exceptions), placing the onus on the delivery teams to anticipate operational needs, not just react to them.

5. Escalation and Stakeholder Management:
- He is insistent on disciplined escalation when obligations from partners or vendors are unmet, specifying exactly who to escalate to and not accepting partial or informal resolutions; this is delivered with a tone of accountability and expectation of systematic follow-through.

6. Scaling Automation and Narrative Ownership:
- Ramki directs that successful templates (e.g., automation in recon) must be extended—he frames learnings as assets for system-wide replication. He also positions Zeta’s approach as something to be shared, codified, and demonstrably differentiated (routine blog posts, stakeholder comms).

- VI. Ramki's Suggested Action Items

1. Platform Support Services Team Description Finalization
- Task: Finalize and share detailed observations and revised job descriptions for the platform support services team, covering new responsibilities and potential overlaps.
- Assigned To: Uttam Rao & Deepak
- Instructions: Observations to cover team incubation/location, clear responsibility demarcation. Document roles beyond troubleshooting. Share this week.
- Timeline: By end of current week

2. Statement Module Decoupling and Lifecycle Expansion
- Task: Redesign the statements module so that it is fully independent (decoupled from Pixel), with complete lifecycle management (generation, delivery, bounce/exception tracking, systemic translation of exception scenarios).
- Assigned To: Abhijit Arkalgud, in consultation with Karthik Raman and Puneeth
- Instructions: Ensure zero Pixel code in the statement path; module must handle end-to-end delivery and post-delivery exception handling as part of its core. Immediate enablement for statement redispatch. Use Tachyon + Luminos only.
- Timeline: Immediate trigger; action to begin this quarter.

3. OPS Center: Daily Checklist/Product Metric Review Implementation
- Task: Build daily operational checklist and daily product metric review, surfaced in OPS Center for all operational reviews (replace Excel, enable system acknowledgment).
- Assigned To: Uttam Rao, Vinay Murthy, Vivekanand, Abhijit Arkalgud
- Instructions: All operational reviews/checks to appear in OPS Center; numbers automated, reviews acknowledged by responsible staff. The design should be client-agnostic, templatable across all products.
- Timeline: Unspecified, but indicated as immediate/fundamental.

4. Reporting and Data Visualization Enhancements (Bank Updates)
- Task: Include logical funnel state, aging (max/min), visual progression, and green-banding in bank-facing status reports. Share updated visualizations with Ramki before client dissemination.
- Assigned To: Uttam Rao, Ashrith
- Instructions: Status visualization to be sequential, clear, and highlight completion/progress; aging must be tracked from prioritization/clarification onward.
- Timeline: Update in next client/bank communication cycle.

5. Exception Escalation for Service Unavailability (e.g., UPI Reversals)
- Task: Ensure escalations for exception/incident scenarios (e.g., delayed reversals) reach the appropriate senior stakeholders (Suresh Vadrajan, Alok, etc.) and are acknowledged formally.
- Assigned To: Uttam Rao, Rohit Dake
- Instructions: Move beyond working-level messages; track who has acknowledged. Formalize process to escalate as per hierarchy until vendor obligations are confirmed.
- Timeline: Immediate; in all cases of ongoing/repeat incidents.

6. Recon Module Enhancements and Documentation
- Task: Extend recon module enhancements (automation, exception handling, support for queering actions beyond match-off), align with utility console/product documentation. Conceptual/theory walkthrough to be recorded and documented.
- Assigned To: Ashish Patel, Parmita, Uttam Rao, Vinay Murthy
- Instructions: Reusable template, ensure "write-off" paths are handled, align terminology and approach for audits. Theory and rationale to be part of official product documentation/demos.
- Timeline: Process initiated; target to present/demonstrate this week.

- VII. Jira Ticket

Possible Jira Tickets:

1. OPS Center Daily Review Automation
- Scope: Create system-supported daily operational checklist and product metric review functionality in OPS Center, including automation and user acknowledgment features, templatized for all products/clients.
- Assignee: OPS Center Platform Team (Vinay Murthy as anchor)
- Justification: High operational impact; replaces error-prone manual Excel checks, enforces process discipline and auditability as directed by Ramki.

2. Statement Module Decoupling & Lifecycle Extension
- Scope: Redesign statements module to operate independently of Pixel, extend lifecycle management features per Ramki’s specification.
- Assignee: Statement Module Owners (Abhijit Arkalgud in technical lead, Karthik Raman for Pixel coordination)
- Justification: Critical modularization goal, removes dependency bottlenecks, and is core to future system resilience.

3. Recon Module Enhancements and SOP/Scenario Documentation
- Scope: In-platform enhancements for recon modules (support for actions beyond match-off, templated extensions, scenario documentation workflow, theory walkthrough).
- Assignee: Recon Product Owners (Ashish Patel, Parmita, with coordination from Uttam Rao)
- Justification: Fundamental platform improvement, aligns with regulatory/SOC audit findings and operational needs as articulated by Ramki.

> Note: Not every action item above would result in a Jira ticket. For example, team structure documentation/sharing and stakeholder escalations are process/communication actions, best tracked outside of Jira.

- VIII. Follow Up Questions/Suggestions

- Schedule a walkthrough for Ramki on the full manual-to-automated UPI reconciliation process, including detailed demonstration of file pickup, decryption, upload, exception handling, and aging report generation.
- Ensure all stakeholders, especially those newly assigned responsibilities (e.g., Vinay for Love Letter/UPI), have explicit knowledge transitions and clarity of scope.
- Prepare a video or written conceptual documentation (as recommended by Ramki) capturing the underlying operational theory, approach, and terminology for scenario-based workflows and recon modules.
- Proactively identify additional areas in reporting and operational dashboards where client-facing visuals (funnel states, aging, completion metrics) can be standardized, reducing ambiguity in client/BOM communication.
title: 2025-04-14_Processing Services Check-in (6)
